root_motivation: The shared cargo target hold (~/wintermute/.hold/target) grew unbounded to 214G and filled the disk; hold-guard enforces a size cap with LRU eviction so the commons can never silently accrete to that state again.
user_persona: Joe Yen — laptop operator running a fleet of Rust builds via the wintermute/hold shared cargo target; runs hold-guard as a maintenance CLI or cron-style enforcer.

acceptance_criteria.description: hold-guard --help lists check, enforce, status subcommands and exits 0.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs: invoke binary with --help, assert exit 0, assert output contains 'check', 'enforce', 'status'.

acceptance_criteria.description: check --max-size <N> against a fixture hold under the cap reports over_cap: false, selects nothing, reclaims nothing (read-only).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs: create fixture dir with small files, run check --max-size 100M, assert JSON output over_cap=false, selected_units=[]. No files removed.

acceptance_criteria.description: check --max-size <N> against a fixture hold over the cap reports over_cap: true and lists LRU units would evict oldest-access first until projected size is below --low-water.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs: create fixture dir over cap with staggered mtimes, run check --max-size, assert over_cap=true and selected units are oldest-first, projected size below low-water.

acceptance_criteria.description: enforce --apply removes exactly the selected LRU units, reports reclaimed_bytes > 0, brings hold below --low-water, appends ledger lines.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs: create fixture over cap, enforce --apply, assert dirs removed, reclaimed_bytes > 0, hold_bytes < low_water, ledger file has new lines.

acceptance_criteria.description: The emitted event validates against ballast-guard's event schema (kind, severity, hold_bytes, cap_bytes, reclaimed_bytes, ts fields present with correct types).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs: run enforce --apply on fixture, parse JSON event output, assert required fields present with correct types.

acceptance_criteria.description: A fingerprint unit with a simulated held lock / open fd is never selected for eviction even when it is the LRU candidate.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs: create fixture where oldest unit has a .cargo-lock file, run enforce --apply, assert that locked unit is not evicted.

acceptance_criteria.description: enforce is dry-run unless --apply is given: without --apply it reclaims nothing and writes no ledger line.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs: run enforce WITHOUT --apply on over-cap fixture, assert no files removed, no ledger written.

acceptance_criteria.description: The ledger is append-only across runs: line count only grows.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac8.rs: run enforce --apply twice, assert ledger line count after second run > after first run.

acceptance_criteria.description: --ts <rfc3339> makes event and ledger timestamps deterministic (no wall-clock in tests).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac9.rs: run enforce --apply --ts 2026-01-01T00:00:00Z, assert event.ts and ledger line ts both equal the provided value.

scope: Measure the size of a cargo target hold directory
LRU-evict fingerprint subdirectories when over a configured cap
Emit structured SLO events matching ballast-guard's event schema
non_goals: Network transport for events (stdout / file only)
Managing anything outside the configured hold path
Daemon / filesystem-watch mode (run as cron or oneshot)

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the hold need a size cap?
five_whys_trace.a: Without a cap the shared cargo target hold grew to 214G and filled the disk to 97%.

five_whys_trace.q: Why did no existing tool prevent the 214G accretion?
five_whys_trace.a: ballast-guard caps whole-disk fossils; hold-anchor+hold-migrate dedupe across machines — but neither bounds the hold's own budget as new dependency versions accrete.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not just run cargo clean periodically?
five_whys_trace.a: cargo clean drops everything; LRU eviction keeps hot artifacts warm (most-recently-built crates survive), so builds stay fast while space is controlled.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must the eviction unit be the per-fingerprint subdirectory?
five_whys_trace.a: cargo's target layout stores per-fingerprint artifacts under target/<profile>/ with stable subdir naming, and mtime reflects last use — making LRU by fingerprint dir both possible and safe (cargo rebuilds an evicted artifact on next use).

five_whys_trace.q: Why reuse ballast-guard's event schema rather than invent a new one?
five_whys_trace.a: A single notifier can surface both whole-disk SLO events and hold-budget events without bespoke glue; composability reduces surface area.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What counts as an 'eviction unit'?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Per-fingerprint subdirectories under deps/, .fingerprint/, and incremental/ within the hold's target directory.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How to detect a held lock?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Check for .cargo-lock file in the unit directory (same approach as hold-migrate). Open-fd check is advisory, not required for v1.
